nativeRange.habitat: Outer reef areas, typically at exposed reef slopes between roughly 10–50 m depth; reported to occur in aggregations
nativeRange.biotope: Tropical Western Pacific reef slopes; associated with outer reef and rubble zones rather than sheltered lagoons

diet.notes: Generally accepts vitamin-enriched frozen mysis shrimp, frozen brine shrimp, and other meaty foods; has been reported to also take high-quality marine flake and pellet food. In the wild, tends to forage as a planktivore/carnivore above the reef substrate in aggregations.

avoidWith: other male fairy wrasses in the same tank

summary: Katherine's Fairy Wrasse (Cirrhilabrus katherinae) is a small, peaceful fish from exposed reef slopes of the Western Pacific. Females and juveniles are orange-red; adult males develop vivid blue-banded fins. Generally considered reef-safe.
careNotes:
Katherine's Fairy Wrasse is found on exposed outer reef slopes in the Western Pacific, ranging from the Izu Islands and Miyake-jima in Japan through Guam and the Mariana Islands; trade specimens are also reported from the Philippines. It typically inhabits depths between 10 and 50 m, often occurring in loose aggregations above the reef substrate.

A tightly fitted lid or eurobrace (a frame of plastic or glass bracing the top of an open aquarium) is essential, as this species, like most fairy wrasses, has been known to jump. The Katherine's Fairy Wrasse is best kept as a single male or as one male alongside one or more females introduced simultaneously to establish a harem; adding females after the male is already settled may provoke territorial behavior.

Females and juveniles are nearly uniformly orange-red, while males develop elongate pelvic fins and brilliant blue bands on the dorsal and anal fins, making it straightforward to tell the sexes apart. Distinct pairing behavior has been documented during breeding. Like all members of the genus Cirrhilabrus, this species is a protogynous hermaphrodite — meaning individuals begin life as females and the dominant fish may later change sex to male — and can maintain harems in larger aquarium systems.
breedingNotes: Captive breeding is not commonly reported. Like most fairy wrasses, haremic spawning with a dominant male is expected; replicating this social structure in a large reef system may encourage nuptial display behavior.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Species product page (currently sold out) confirmed: care level easy, temperament peaceful, reef compatible yes, minimum tank 90 gal, temperature 72–78°F, SG 1.020–1.025, pH 8.1–8.4, dKH 8–12, diet carnivore (mysis/brine/meaty foods + marine flake/pellet), adult size 4 in, native locale Philippines. Confirmed a covered tank is required due to jumping behavior.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked: max size 7.1 cm SL, native range Western Pacific (Izu Islands Japan, Guam, Mariana Islands), depth 10–50 m outer reef, temperature 25.5–28.9°C (mean 27.7°C), family Labridae, order Eupercaria, occurs in aggregations, distinct pairing during breeding, sexual dichromatism (females/juveniles orange-red; males develop blue-banded fins and elongate pelvic fins). IUCN: Least Concern. Temperature ranges show moderate spread vs LiveAquaria's 22–26°C; averaged to 24–27°C. Reef2Reef wiki returned HTTP 403; BlueZoo Aquatics had no species page for this species — FishBase used as primary cross-verification source.
